DISCOVERY 302, MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER RE: REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 20, 2019 AT 2:00 P.M. FOR A COURT REPORTER. JUDGE PRO TEM: NOAH LEBOWITZ; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. IN THE NORMAL COURSE, A PENDING DEMURRER WOULD NOT BE CAUSE FOR ENTRY OF A PROTECTIVE ORDER. HOWEVER, THE FACTS AND SCENARIO PRESENTED BY THIS CASE LEAD THE COURT TO CONCLUDE THAT A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS APPROPRIATE. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT WILL EXERCISE ITS INHERENT AUTHORITY AND GRANT THE REQUESTED ORDER, TO REMAIN IN PLACE UNTIL SUCH TIME AS THE DEMURRER IS RESOLVED. SHOULD THE DEMURRER ULTIMATELY BE OVERRULED, THE DISCOVERY WILL BE DEEMED SERVED AS OF THE DATE OF THAT RULING. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: NOAH LEBOWITZ; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. Defendant Caitlin Barker, Julia Lemke, Richard Wood, Bryan Quintero, Chad Bank, Rachel Amalia, Talia Khayat Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JUSTIN A. GOODMAN, Esq. of Zacks, Freedman & Patterson, PC (Tel: 415-956-8100) for Plaintiff Fillmore Manor, LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: Defendants Caitlin Barker, Julia Lemke, Richard Wood, Bryan Quintero, Chad Bank, Rachel Amalia, and Talia Khayat's Demurrer To Complaint.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Court declines to exercise its discretion to dismiss Plaintiff's Complaint. (Code Civ. Proc., section 1061.) Plaintiff sufficiently pleads a cause of action for declaratory relief. (Code Civ. Proc., section 1060.) Plaintiff alleges that an actual controversy exists, which requires a judicial determination regarding the rights and obligations of the parties pursuant to the lease agreement and the Ellis Act. As such, Plaintiff is entitled to seek declaratory relief. (Qualified Patients Ass'n v. City of Anaheim (2010) 187 Cal.App.4th 734, 751.) No other action between the parties is pending before this Court or the Rent Board. (Cf. General of America Ins. Co. v. Lilly (1968) 258 Cal.App.2d 465, 471 [concluding that the plaintiff's declaratory relief claim could be resolved in the main personal injury action].) Defendants' arguments that Plaintiff's Complaint should be dismissed pending the outcome of Plaintiff's First Supplemental Petition are unpersuasive because Plaintiff is not seeking a judicial determination regarding a rent increase here. (Complaint, pp. 7-8.) Defendants also fail to demonstrate why the Rent Board is a more appropriate forum when Plaintiff is not seeking a judicial declaration regarding a rent increase. (See SFRO section 37.8(b)(1); DeLaura v. Beckett (2006) 137 Cal.App.4th 542, 546-548 [finding "a more appropriate statutory procedure" pursuant to SFRO section 37.9(i)(4) to determine whether the defendant had protected status.].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Real Party In Interest Chad Bank Demurrer
Off Calendar Oct-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JUSTIN A. GOODMAN, Esq. of Zacks, Freedman & Patterson, PC (Tel: 415-956-8100) for Plaintiff Fillmore Manor, LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: Real Party In Interest Chad Bank's Demurrer.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Real Party in Interest Chad Bank's Demurrer is overruled.

Petitioner alleges facts sufficient to state a cause of action for declaratory relief pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1060. Petitioner alleges that the parties dispute whether Petitioner can use a notice pursuant to Civil Code section 827 to change the terms of the tenancy. (Petition, 21.) Petitioner further alleges that a judicial determination of the parties' rights and obligations is appropriate. (Petition, 24.) Petitioner is not required to allege that Respondent exceeded its jurisdiction when seeking declaratory relief. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 1060.) The Real Party in Interest's Demurrer is overruled on this ground.

Petitioner also alleges facts sufficient to state a cause of action for Administrative Mandamus pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1094.5. Petitioner alleges that Respondent issued a decision on January 7, 2020, which found that Petitioner's Civil Code section 827 Notice constituted an improper rent increase. (Petition, 25.) Petitioner alleges that the Respondent exceeded its jurisdiction by voiding Petitioner's Civil Code section 827 Notice. (Petition, 27.) Petitioner is not required to allege evidentiary facts to support factual allegations. (Birke v. Oakwood Worldwide (2009) 169 Cal.App.4th 1540, 1548-1553 [finding that general allegations of ultimate fact can survive demurrer as a plaintiff is not required to plead evidentiary facts].) Real Party in Interest's Demurrer is overruled on this ground.

Real Party in Interest lacks standing to challenge the sufficiency of the third cause of action for Writ of Prohibition pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1102. Petitioner pleads the third cause of action against Respondents only. (Petition, 32-39.) Real Party in Interest's Demurrer is overruled on this ground.

Order signed.
